reasoning: Tajima Beef appears as the first item with an authoritative MAFF GI description, followed immediately by Kobe Beef showing the parent-child relationship. All data is from official GI registration with registration number, dates, and producer group. The query's key insight (Tajima as origin of Kobe) is directly answerable from the first two records.
missing_data: No tasting locations, restaurants, or where-to-try guidance. No price range or seasonality notes for the traveler.
improvement_hint: Pin 'Tajima Beef' and 'Kobe Beef' as the first two results regardless of list ordering when prefecture=Hyogo to ensure the parent-child relationship is immediately visible.

reasoning: Identical content to L1-01 but in Arabic. لحم بقر تاجيما and لحم بقر كوبي appear first with high-confidence official translations. Arabic description text is accurate and fluent. translation_meta confidence=high for both key items. Third item (Sayo mochi soybean) is unrelated to the query but expected in a full-prefecture list.
missing_data: No Arabic-language tasting venue data. No price or seasonal guidance.
improvement_hint: When query intent targets a named GI product, rank exact-name matches above alphabetical/registration-number order for any language.

reasoning: Query explicitly asks about Minami-Yamashiro village tea fields, not Uji. The tool returned Kyoto Japan Heritage items dominated by Nijo Castle, Toji, and Nishi Honganji — all major Kyoto city landmarks with zero relevance to Minami-Yamashiro or tea cultivation. The tool scope note flags this tool is wrong for the intent, but no redirect occurred.
missing_data: Any data about Minami-Yamashiro village (南山城村), its tea cultivation landscape, tea fields, DMO pages, or Yamashiro tea terroir.
wrong_data: All results are Kyoto city UNESCO WHS properties unrelated to Minami-Yamashiro tea.
improvement_hint: Route municipality-specific queries through get_spots or search_area with municipality param. Japan Heritage tool is wrong for village-level landscape queries.

reasoning: The exact festival (吉田の火祭) appears as first item with the Spanish name, designation, and correct location (Fujiyoshida). However description is minimal — just city and prefecture. The national_heritage section includes geographically irrelevant items from Akita and Saitama. Date field is null. No dates, schedule, or visitor guidance.
missing_data: Festival date (August 26-27), significance as closing ceremony of Mt Fuji climbing season, crowd/access tips, link to Fujiyoshida tourism.
improvement_hint: Enrich bunka_intangible records with festival dates from Wikidata P585/P580 fields; filter national_heritage to Yamanashi-relevant items only.

reasoning: Query asks specifically about Chugei-region yuzu (中芸ゆず). First item is 物部ゆず (Monobe yuzu, Kami city) which is adjacent but not Chugei. 中芸ゆず as a GI or named product is not visible. The second item is a scraped tourist spot listing (nav chrome, no food relevance). 500-item truncation means the specific product may exist but is buried.
missing_data: 中芸ゆず specifically (Nahari, Kitagawa, Umaji, Geisei, Toyo villages), yuzu processing facilities, local products using Chugei yuzu.
wrong_data: Second scraped item is pure navigation chrome about Kochi sightseeing, not food data.
improvement_hint: Add municipality-level keyword filter to get_local_food so 'Chugei' or '中芸' narrows results; scraper should tag yuzu by sub-regional origin.

reasoning: Both Hirosaki Castle and Hirosaki Park (with sakura_meisho_100 kind) appear in top results. The park is the exact intent target. Coordinates, opening hours, phone, and website are present. Heritage designations confirmed. However lang is not specified as zh and Chinese names are sparsely populated (name_zh present for castle but vacant for several spots).
missing_data: Cherry blossom bloom period (late April), festival dates, parking and access from Hirosaki Station.
improvement_hint: Pre-populate name_zh and description_zh for major sakura spots ahead of spring season; ensure lang=zh is correctly applied when query is in Chinese.

reasoning: Izumo Taisha appears first with National Treasure designation, coordinates, website, opening hours. Main hall and access station also present. However Korean translations are absent — names are returned in Japanese/English despite lang=ko. The Chiba branch (出雲大社上総分院) appearing in results is noise.
missing_data: Korean-language name fields, description in Korean. Details on the deity (Okuninushi) and marriage-connection significance which drives Korean tourist interest.
improvement_hint: Ensure Korean name_ko field is populated for top-tier shrines; filter branch-shrine results when canonical main shrine is in a different prefecture from the query.

reasoning: Itsukushima Shrine appears as the second result (below the island itself) with UNESCO WHS, National Treasure, website, and coordinates. The torii gate (Ootorii) also appears. Data is accurate and comprehensive. However lang=en was used for a French-language query — response lacks French translations.
missing_data: French-language descriptions. Ferry access info from Miyajimaguchi, tidal schedule for walking to the torii.
improvement_hint: When query language is French, pass lang=fr to the tool. Add tidal schedule note or link for Miyajima torii low-tide walking.

reasoning: Kumano Kodo appears as the third wikidata_heritage item with correct UNESCO designation and coordinates. Kumano Sanzan also present. Tool scope note correctly warns this tool is for 日本遺産 not UNESCO WHS, yet the canonical heritage section properly surfaces Kumano Kodo. German-language query returns English — lang mismatch. Koyasan and Negoro-ji add relevant context.
missing_data: German-language descriptions. Route options (Nakahechi, Kohechi), trailhead access, required fitness level, Oji staging posts.
improvement_hint: Pass lang=de for German queries. Add pilgrimage route specifics (trailhead towns, route names, distance) to wikidata_heritage entries with kaido/pilgrimage_route kinds.

reasoning: Himeji Castle appears first with UNESCO WHS, National Treasure, Special Historic Site designations, precise opening hours (seasonal), wheelchair accessibility (no), phone, website, price_band. All factually accurate. Secondary results are sub-structures of the castle complex (Ni Watariyagura etc.) which are relevant. Lang mismatch (Russian query → en response) is the only notable gap.
missing_data: Russian-language descriptions. Seasonal cherry blossom viewing context (Himeji also has sakura_meisho_100).
improvement_hint: Pass lang=ru for Russian queries. Ensure sakura_meisho_100 kind is surfaced in spring-related queries for Himeji.

reasoning: Bunraku appears first in Thai with correct UNESCO ICH designation (2003). Art form description is accurate. However query asks specifically about seeing Bunraku in Osaka — no venue data (National Bunraku Theatre address, schedule, ticket info). Following items (Kabuki, Chakkirako, etc.) are unrelated to Osaka or Bunraku.
missing_data: 国立文楽劇場 (National Bunraku Theatre) in Osaka, address, performance schedule, ticket prices, how to book.
improvement_hint: Cross-link traditional arts items to Wikidata venue entities (e.g., 国立文楽劇場 Q11305261) so the agent can provide location/ticket guidance.

reasoning: Query asks specifically about Yumihama Kasuri (弓浜絣), a METI-designated traditional indigo kasuri weave from Tottori. The tool returned 7 food GI items (rakkyo, broccoli, persimmon, watermelon) with no craft items visible. 弓浜絣 exists as meti_densan craft but was not retrieved — possible tool call without category=craft filter, or the craft data is missing from Tottori.
missing_data: 弓浜絣 METI craft record, production area (Yonago/Sakaiminato region), workshop/museum where it can be seen, weaving technique description.
improvement_hint: get_local_specialty without category filter should return both food and craft items. Verify 弓浜絣 is present in meti_densan data for Tottori (prefecture_code 31).

reasoning: Benesse Art Site Naoshima appears first with website and fee. New Museum of Art second. Municipality entity and scraped tourism-association spots follow. Chichu Art Museum (Tadao Ando) absent. Lang mismatch (Tagalog query → en response). Scraped spots include Chinese-language tourism pages which add noise rather than Tagalog/English content.
missing_data: 地中美術館 (Chichu Art Museum), 家プロジェクト (Art House Project), ferry access from Uno/Takamatsu, island cycle route.
improvement_hint: Index Chichu Art Museum and Art House Project as must_see Wikidata entities for Naoshima. Scraped duplicate spots (same URL, different language) should be deduplicated.

reasoning: Kakunodate samurai district appears first with a rich description mentioning weeping cherry blossoms, Important Preservation District designation, coordinates. Kakunodate town entity second, weeping cherry trees third with Natural Monument designation. Hirafuku Memorial Art Museum and Kakunodate Castle also present. Comprehensive, accurate, well-structured.
missing_data: Individual samurai house names open to public (e.g., Aoyagi-ke, Iwahashi-ke), admission fees, access from Akita city.
improvement_hint: Add individual buke-yashiki (武家屋敷) Wikidata entities for open residences; they are key visitor attractions not yet separately indexed.

reasoning: Shimanami Kaido appears first and is highly relevant as Onomichi's most famous attraction. Art museum, city entity present. However the old town temple walk, cat alley (猫の細道), Senko-ji (千光寺), and ropeways — the core Onomichi experience — are absent. Response gives a thin picture of Onomichi's walkable historic town character. Lang mismatch (Indonesian → en).
missing_data: 千光寺 (Senkoji), 猫の細道 (Cat Alley), ロープウェイ (ropeway), 浄土寺, the hillside temple walk circuit that defines the Onomichi visitor experience.
improvement_hint: Scrape or add Wikidata entities for Senkoji and the Onomichi temple walk; they are repeatedly cited in DMO content but absent from the corpus.

reasoning: Nachi Falls appears first with Place of Scenic Beauty and Natural Monument designations, coordinates, typical visit time. Adjacent Nachi Primeval Forest and 48 Waterfalls also present. '那智滝図' (a painting in a Tokyo museum) appears in results with must_see tier — misleading in a travel context. Lang mismatch (Spanish → en). No Kumano Nachi Taisha context which is essential to a visit.
missing_data: Kumano Nachi Taisha (shrine adjacent to falls), access from Kii-Katsuura Station, entrance fee for Hiro Jinja viewing platform.
wrong_data: 那智滝図 (a painting item, prefecture_code 13/Tokyo) ranked must_see in a Wakayama falls query is misleading noise.
improvement_hint: Filter out art object / painting items (kinds=null, coordinate in different prefecture) from geographic attraction queries. Add Kumano Nachi Taisha as companion entity to Nachi Falls.

reasoning: Shiretoko Peninsula appears in the top 2 results with UNESCO WHS designation. National Park, Mount Io, World Heritage Conservation Center all follow. Good multi-angle coverage of the peninsula. Lang mismatch (Hindi query → en). No seasonal access warnings (road closures, bear warnings April-November).
missing_data: Seasonal access notes (Shiretoko Pass closes in winter), bear activity warnings, Rausu vs. Utoro side choices, recommended tour operators for drift ice.
improvement_hint: Add a seasonal_note field to Shiretoko entities flagging winter/spring road closures. Pass lang=hi when query is in Hindi.

reasoning: Three Mountains of Dewa (出羽三山) appears first correctly. However the next results include unrelated items: 出羽島 (Tokushima), 出羽桜美術館 (Yamagata sake museum), 出羽山地 (Aomori mountain range). The signal-to-noise ratio is poor after the first hit. Dewa Sanzan Shrine appears fifth with no coordinates.
missing_data: The three individual peaks (Haguro-san, Gas-san, Yudono-san) as separate entities with their shrine details, pilgrim mountain-entry regulations, yamabushi ascetic routes.
improvement_hint: For proper-noun landmark queries, boost prefecture-matching results over phonetic coincidences. Index Haguro-san, Gas-san, and Yudono-san as individual entities linked to 出羽三山.

reasoning: Yakushima island appears first with UNESCO WHS, Wilderness Area, and Ramsar designations. National Park, World Heritage Center, History Museum, and main shrine also present. Comprehensive and accurate. Lang mismatch (Portuguese → en). Jomon Sugi and Shiratani Unsuikyo — the two iconic trekking sites — are not visible in the truncated results.
missing_data: 縄文杉 (Jomon Cedar) as a named entity, 白谷雲水峡 (Shiratani Unsuikyo), recommended trekking durations, ferry access from Kagoshima.
improvement_hint: Ensure Jomon Sugi (Q11302982) and Shiratani Unsuikyo are indexed as must_see Yakushima attractions; they are the primary reason visitors travel to the island.

reasoning: Query is broadly about Sado Island. Tool returned Japan Heritage stories — Sado Mine (佐渡金山) is a valid top hit with UNESCO WHS. But the Japan Heritage stories are dominated by 信濃川流域 fire-shaped pottery, which is Niigata mainland — not Sado island. The tool_scope_note warns about this mismatch but the agent did not redirect. Korean names are present where data allows.
missing_data: Sado Island as a geographic entity, toki (crested ibis) sanctuary, ondeko drumming, Kitazawa Flotation Plant ruins, ferry access from Niigata.
improvement_hint: For island-entity queries, prefer search_area or get_spots with municipality='佐渡市' over Japan Heritage tool. Link 佐渡金山 UNESCO WHS entity to the Sado Island entity.

reasoning: Query asks about Tohoku region broadly (6 prefectures). Tool was called with only Akita, returning 日本秘湯を守る会 members from Akita alone. These are legitimate traditional onsen ryokan, but Tohoku includes Aomori, Iwate, Miyagi, Yamagata, and Fukushima which have famous secret hot springs (Nyuto Onsen, Osorezan, etc.). Constraint (Tohoku) is only partially addressed.
missing_data: Nyuto Onsen (Akita/Iwate border), Kaminoyu Onsen, Zao Onsen (Yamagata), Namari Onsen (Iwate), other Tohoku prefectures' 秘湯 members.
improvement_hint: When query specifies a region (Tohoku/Kanto/etc.), call get_hotels for all constituent prefectures and merge results. Add region parameter to get_hotels.

reasoning: Query specifically asks for 宿坊 (shukubo — temple lodging) on the Shikoku Ohenro pilgrimage. The tool returned generic Tokushima hotels and ryokan (guest houses, business hotels) with no shukubo. Zero pilgrimage-specific lodging options. The 88-temple pilgrimage route is the defining Shikoku experience but temple lodging data appears absent from the corpus.
missing_data: Any of the 88 Shikoku Henro temples offering shukubo accommodation, especially Ryozenji (Temple 1), Gokurakuji (Temple 2), price range (¥6,000-¥8,000/night typical), booking method.
improvement_hint: Scrape official 四国八十八ヶ所霊場会 website for temple lodging (宿坊) listings and add lodging_type=shukubo to hotels data.

reasoning: Query asks for 農業体験 (farm experience) in Hokkaido. Tool returned get_spots with general tourist attractions: Showa-shinzan volcano, Sapporo Clock Tower, Matsumae Castle. Zero farm experience results. The query intent is agritourism (lavender farms, dairy farms, strawberry picking) which the general spots tool cannot satisfy and the corpus appears to lack.
missing_data: Farm Tomita (lavender, Furano), farm stay / agritourism operators, Tokachi agricultural experience programs, dairy/cheese farm tours, vegetable picking programs.
improvement_hint: Add agritourism/farm-experience as a kind_tag and scrape official Hokkaido agritourism registries (e.g., Hokkaido Tourism Organization farm-stay pages).

reasoning: Imari-Arita Ware and Karatsu Ware are correct Kyushu pottery traditions with good METI descriptions. Hakata Ori (weaving) is off-topic for a pottery query. However query asks 'where can I see pottery being made' — i.e., kiln workshops with visitor access. The see_also_wikidata_venues returns a library and museum, not an actual pottery kiln. Correct art forms, wrong layer of information.
missing_data: Arita Porcelain Park, individual kilns (窯元) open to visitors in Arita/Imari/Karatsu, kiln-walk routes, hands-on experience workshops.
improvement_hint: Cross-link METI craft records to get_spots kiln venues (窯元) in the production_area_text municipalities; add kind_tag=kiln_visit for OSM/Wikidata kiln entities.

reasoning: Query asks about Tohoku festivals (6 prefectures). Tool fetched only Aomori, returning 2 items (Hachinohe Sansha Taisai, Aomori Nebuta). National heritage section includes non-Tohoku items (Kawagoe/Saitama, Tatemon/Toyama). Major Tohoku festivals like Sendai Tanabata, Akita Kanto, Yamagata Hanagasa, Morioka Sansa Odori are absent. Korean translations are good for what is present.
missing_data: Sendai Tanabata (Miyagi), Akita Kanto (Akita), Yamagata Hanagasa Matsuri, Morioka Sansa Odori (Iwate) — all UNESCO ICH or Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties.
wrong_data: National heritage items from Saitama and Toyama are irrelevant to a Tohoku festivals query.
improvement_hint: When query specifies Tohoku region, call get_festivals for all 6 prefectures (Aomori/Iwate/Miyagi/Akita/Yamagata/Fukushima) and merge. Add region=tohoku parameter.
